author: l6vc
content:
Fixed in 8f2fe49. Root cause: LockFileEx on Windows locks byte ranges and blocks concurrent opens-for-read of the locked region (unlike flock on Unix which is advisory and read-transparent). The test opened a second handle via fs::read_to_string while the first handle still held the exclusive byte-range lock.

Fix: release the lock before reading the sentinel content. The PID/timestamp written during acquire() persists on disk after drop, so the read-back still validates that acquire populates the diagnostic content — just portably across both locking semantics.
